name: Enchiladas With"refried" Beans and 2 Sauces
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Green bell pepper, finely chopped
2 cups Rinsed and drained (15 oz) Canned Pinto Beans
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 12-inch skillet over medium heat, heat the oil.
2. Add onion, green bell pepper, and garlic; cook for 3 to 4 minutes until softened.
3. Stir in chili powder, cumin, and oregano; cook and stir for 1 minute.
4. Add beans, corn, broth, cilantro, salt, and pepper to the skillet. Bring mixture to a boil, stirring occasionally.
5. Reduce heat to medium-low; cook for 5 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally until most of the liquid has been absorbed.
6. Meanwhile, preheat oven to 350°F. Spray an 12 x 8 x 2-inch baking dish with no-stick cooking spray.
7. Remove skillet from heat. Using the back of a spoon, mash half or all of the beans as desired for texture.
8. Place one warm tortilla on the work surface; spoon 3 to 4 tablespoons bean mixture down the center. Roll up the tortilla carefully.
9. Repeat with remaining tortillas and bean mixture, placing each enchilada in the prepared baking dish.
10. Spoon Salsa Verde or Red Enchilada Sauce over the enchiladas.
11. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes until heated through. Serve immediately, accompanied by sour cream if desired.
